question: Liam has 4 books and buys 3 more. How many books does he have now?
A baker made 12 muffins and sold 5. How many muffins are left?
Nina reads 8 pages on Monday and 9 pages on Tuesday. How many pages did she read in total?
A bus has 18 passengers. At the first stop, 6 get off and 4 get on. How many passengers are on the bus now?
Tom has 3 bags with 4 marbles in each bag. How many marbles does he have altogether?
A teacher puts 20 pencils equally into 4 boxes. How many pencils are in each box?
Maya has 15 candies. She gives 4 to her friend and then buys 6 more. How many candies does Maya have now?
There are 9 birds on a tree. 2 fly away and then 5 more land. How many birds are on the tree now?
If a toy costs 7 dollars and Ben buys 3 toys, how much does he spend?
A rope is 30 meters long. It is cut into 3 equal pieces. How long is each piece?
Sara has twice as many stickers as Leo. Leo has 6 stickers. How many stickers does Sara have?
A farm has 5 chickens and 3 cows. How many legs are there in total?
John is older than Mia. Mia is older than Ava. Who is the oldest?
There are 24 cookies. They are shared equally among 6 children. How many cookies does each child get?
A shop sold 11 pens in the morning and 8 pens in the afternoon. Then 3 pen orders were canceled. How many pens were sold in the end?
Emma has 10 coins. She finds 5 more and loses 3. How many coins does she have now?
Two boxes each contain 9 oranges. If 4 oranges are eaten from one box, how many oranges remain in total?
A class has 14 girls and 13 boys. How many students are in the class?
If 36 pages are read over 4 days equally, how many pages are read each day?
Olivia has 2 red ribbons and 5 blue ribbons. She buys 3 more red ribbons. How many red ribbons does she have now?
